# Sensitivity Analysis — Framework v0.6

*Generated by `scripts/sensitivity_analysis.py` on 2026-05-02.*

Each of the 11 dimension weights is perturbed at +-2.5% and +-5% (4 perturbations per dimension, 44 scenarios total). The remaining weights are rescaled proportionally so total weight stays at 100%. The headline weighted ranking is recomputed under each scenario and compared to the baseline.

The point is not to declare any ranking objective — it is to make explicit which conclusions survive reasonable disagreement on weights, and which do not.

## Baseline

| Rank | Language | Weighted score |
|---:|---|---:|
| 1 | Go | 4.01 |
| 1 | TypeScript | 4.01 |
| 3 | .NET | 3.99 |
| 4 | Kotlin | 3.85 |
| 5 | Python | 3.71 |
| 5 | Rust | 3.71 |
| 7 | Java | 3.50 |
| 8 | Swift | 3.42 |
| 9 | Elixir | 3.10 |
| 10 | C++ | 2.46 |

## Per-language stability band

| Language | Baseline | Min rank | Max rank | Held |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Go | 1 | 1 | 3 | 22/44 |
| TypeScript | 1 | 1 | 3 | 27/44 |
| .NET | 3 | 1 | 3 | 27/44 |
| Kotlin | 4 | 4 | 4 | 44/44 |
| Python | 5 | 5 | 6 | 28/44 |
| Rust | 5 | 5 | 6 | 28/44 |
| Java | 7 | 7 | 8 | 42/44 |
| Swift | 8 | 7 | 8 | 41/44 |
| Elixir | 9 | 9 | 9 | 44/44 |
| C++ | 10 | 10 | 10 | 44/44 |

## Robust findings

A finding is "robust" if the language's rank holds across every one of the 44 perturbation scenarios (no flip, anywhere, at any +-2.5% or +-5% shift).

- **Kotlin at rank 4** — holds rank in all 44 perturbations (range 4-4).
- **Elixir at rank 9** — holds rank in all 44 perturbations (range 9-9).
- **C++ at rank 10** — holds rank in all 44 perturbations (range 10-10).

## Fragile findings

A finding is "fragile" if the language's rank flips with at least one neighbor under some perturbation. Smaller flip thresholds mean more fragility; the listed flip is the smallest-magnitude perturbation that moved this language away from its baseline rank.

- **Go at rank 1** — held in 22/44 perturbations; range 1-3; first flip at Human cognition (HC) -2.5% (moves to rank 3).
- **TypeScript at rank 1** — held in 27/44 perturbations; range 1-3; first flip at Human cognition (HC) +2.5% (moves to rank 2).
- **.NET at rank 3** — held in 27/44 perturbations; range 1-3; first flip at Human cognition (HC) -2.5% (moves to rank 2).
- **Python at rank 5** — held in 28/44 perturbations; range 5-6; first flip at Human cognition (HC) -2.5% (moves to rank 6).
- **Rust at rank 5** — held in 28/44 perturbations; range 5-6; first flip at Human cognition (HC) +2.5% (moves to rank 6).
- **Java at rank 7** — held in 42/44 perturbations; range 7-8; first flip at AI-systems native (AIN) +5.0% (moves to rank 8).
- **Swift at rank 8** — held in 41/44 perturbations; range 7-8; first flip at AI-systems native (AIN) +5.0% (moves to rank 7).

## Per-dimension fragility map

Which languages move rank when each dimension's weight is perturbed?

| Dimension | Languages whose rank flips under +-5% |
|---|---|
| Human cognition (HC) | .NET, Go, Python, Rust, TypeScript |
| Machine cognition (MC) | Python, Rust |
| AI-agent operability (AO) | .NET, Go, TypeScript |
| Runtime / ecosystem (RE) | Python, Rust |
| Strategic viability (SV) | Python, Rust |
| AI-systems native (AIN) | .NET, Go, Java, Swift, TypeScript |
| AI-systems ecosystem (AIE) | .NET, Go, Java, Python, Rust, Swift, TypeScript |
| Structured-output native (SON) | .NET, Go, Python, Rust, TypeScript |
| Structured-output ecosystem (SOE) | .NET, Go, Python, Rust, Swift, TypeScript |
| Ecosystem dependency risk (EDR) | .NET, Go, Python, Rust, TypeScript |
| Reachability to top tier (Reach) | .NET, Go, TypeScript |

## How to read this

A "robust" finding can be reported as a fact at v0.6 confidence: it survives every reasonable disagreement about how heavily to weight each dimension. A "fragile" finding should be reported with the perturbation footnote: "this rank holds at the chosen weights; under a +-X% shift on dimension Y, this language and Z swap." Reach/EDR/SV at 5% each are the most easily perturbed knobs; AO at 20% is the most load-bearing.

The robustness band published here is what v0.7 multi-rater work and v0.9 external review will adjust as the weights themselves come under scrutiny.
